strategic_profile.executive_summary: UC Davis is a top-tier public land-grant research university renowned globally for its world-class veterinary medicine, agriculture, and biological sciences programs [cite: 1, 2, 3]. It offers rigorous academics within a highly collaborative, friendly, and quintessential bike-friendly college town environment [cite: 4, 5].
strategic_profile.market_position: Public Ivy / Elite Public Research University
strategic_profile.admissions_philosophy: Holistic review prioritizing academic rigor, capped weighted GPA, and personal context, operating under a strict test-blind policy [cite: 6, 7, 8].

analyst_takeaways.insight: UC Davis holds the global #1 position in Veterinary Medicine and Agriculture, making related undergraduate programs exceptionally strong but demanding [cite: 1, 5].
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students aiming for animal science, agriculture, or biological sciences will find unparalleled research facilities but must survive rigorous early-level STEM courses.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The campus admits heavily off the waitlist, with waitlist admit rates occasionally exceeding 40% [cite: 8].
analyst_takeaways.implication: Waitlisted students have a realistic chance of admission if they opt-in promptly, mitigating the sting of initial deferrals.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Reviewers consistently praise the non-cutthroat, supportive atmosphere and extreme bike-friendliness of the campus [cite: 4, 9].
analyst_takeaways.implication: Excellent fit for high-achieving students seeking a balanced, community-focused college experience over an intense urban pressure-cooker.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: A laid-back, welcoming college town vibe with over 700 student clubs. The community is highly supportive and lacks the hyper-competitive edge found at some sister UC campuses [cite: 4, 5, 10].
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Exceptionally bike-friendly; the campus core is closed to vehicles, and biking is the primary mode of transit [cite: 3, 4].
campus_dynamics.research_impact: As an R1 institution, it offers immense undergraduate research opportunities, particularly in environmental, life, and agricultural sciences [cite: 5, 11, 12].

current_status.test_policy_details: UC Davis is entirely test-blind. SAT and ACT scores are not considered for admission or scholarships, even if submitted [cite: 7, 13].

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist admit rate is calculated based on students who accepted their spot on the waitlist [cite: 8, 14].
Waitlist admit rate calculated based on total offered spots due to missing accepted spots data [cite: 15, 16].

gpa.notes: GPA is heavily weighted in the absence of standardized testing. Most successful applicants have near perfect unweighted GPAs [cite: 17, 18].

testing.policy_note: Test-blind institution. These scores reflect the academic profile of enrolled students who submitted scores for course placement, but were not used in admissions decisions [cite: 7, 19].

colleges.name: College of Agricultural and Environmental Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students passionate about sustainability, animal welfare, and agricultural sciences. Offers a slightly less impacted admissions pathway compared to Engineering [cite: 6, 20].

majors.name: Animal Science
majors.notes: Highly regarded globally. Many students use this major as a rigorous springboard for veterinary school [cite: 21, 22, 23].

colleges.name: College of Letters and Science
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Best for students wanting curricular flexibility, humanities, or theoretical sciences.

majors.name: Psychology
majors.special_requirements: Must achieve a 2.50 GPA combined in PSC 001 and PSC 041 to formally declare [cite: 24].
majors.notes: The B.S. track requires rigorous STEM foundations in chemistry and biology compared to the A.B. track [cite: 24, 25].

colleges.name: College of Engineering
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Highly selective; applicants must have elite math and physics grades and clear alignment with engineering in their PIQs.

majors.name: Computer Science
notable_professors: Raissa M. D'Souza (Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering)
majors.notes: CS at UCD is heavily theoretical and math-intensive, preparing students exceptionally well for graduate study [cite: 26, 27, 28].

colleges.name: College of Biological Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Perfect for pre-meds and aspiring life-science researchers. Highly impacted across all majors.
colleges.student_archetype: Future healthcare professionals and biological researchers.

majors.name: Biological Sciences
majors.notes: Features renowned faculty, including National Academy of Sciences members [cite: 29, 30, 31, 32, 33].

supplemental_requirements.details: Applicants must choose 4 out of 8 Personal Insight Questions (PIQs). Each response is limited to 350 words [cite: 34, 35].

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: With the test-blind policy, UC Davis places immense weight on course rigor (AP/IB/Honors) taken in the 10th and 11th grades, paired with leadership demonstrated through PIQs [cite: 6, 7].

major_selection_tactics: Apply to non-impacted majors within the College of Agricultural and Environmental Sciences to statistically improve admission odds [cite: 6, 36].
If intending to study Computer Science, consider applying to Applied Mathematics or Statistics, though internally transferring to CS later is notoriously difficult [cite: 28, 37].
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: UC Davis considers applicants for their alternate major if the primary major is full. It is highly advised to select an unimpacted alternate major [cite: 6, 37].

financials.tuition_model: Tuition Stability Plan: Systemwide tuition and fees remain flat for up to six years beyond a student's first enrolled term [cite: 38].

scholarships.name: Regents Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Highly prestigious four-year award. Often includes a stipend for demonstrated financial need, plus priority registration [cite: 39, 40].

scholarships.name: Provost Award
scholarships.benefits: Granted to high-achieving out-of-state students to assist with supplemental tuition costs [cite: 39, 41].

ap_policy.general_rule: Scores of 3, 4, or 5 yield 2, 4, or 8 quarter units toward graduation [cite: 42].
exceptions: Maximum of 8 quarter units total for Calculus AB and BC combined [cite: 42].
ap_policy.usage: Degree credit and lower-division prerequisite clearance [cite: 42].

ib_policy.general_rule: Higher Level (HL) exams with scores of 5, 6, or 7 receive 8 quarter units [cite: 43].

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Transfer applicants must complete all major preparatory course requirements by the end of the spring term prior to fall enrollment [cite: 44, 45].

what_it_takes: Rigorous UC-approved A-G course load with maximum AP/Honors classes [cite: 36].
Compelling PIQs that demonstrate leadership, intellectual vitality, and community impact [cite: 34, 35].
common_activities: Participating in research laboratories or assisting faculty [cite: 10].
Leadership in over 700 available student organizations [cite: 4, 10].
Internships in the nearby Sacramento area or in agricultural/environmental sectors [cite: 46].
essay_tips: Ensure responses are self-contained and do not repeat themes across multiple PIQs [cite: 35].
Provide concrete examples of overcoming educational barriers or expanding upon an academic subject that inspires you [cite: 34, 35].
red_flags: Dropping core A-G classes during senior year [cite: 36].
Earning grades below a C in any selective major prerequisite coursework [cite: 44, 45].
insights: Students widely celebrate the friendly, non-competitive culture, which provides a stark contrast to the intense cutthroat environments at other elite UC campuses [cite: 9].
